---
title: "March 2, 2026"
---

# Summary

Random Classic image segmentation techniques (CART, Random Forest, NaiveBayes and SVM) are available for use in Google Earth Engine, on up to 1,000,000 points at a time. 1,000,000 is really not many. That's a 1MP photo, like a 20-year-old phone camera. I used to work with at least 5MP industrial machine vision cameras, and sometimes trained models for those images on a laptop. It seems Google is not being particularly generous with the cloud computing resources, but it should at least be fast to train.

# Applications

\@choudhary_random_2022 trained a Random Forest model on Sentinel-2 data from GEE to predict rice yield from small farms in Guangdong Province, China. Lots of field work was involved in collecting the actual status of the rice fields, which was supported by the Guangdong Department of Agriculture. They used much more than 1000000 points and had to train the model outside of GEE, but achieved much more accurate results on test data than linear or single decision tree models.

# Reflection

This sort of approach seems pretty straightforward and foolproof.
